briefing:
## 🌍 Top 5 Global Fintech News

- **[Fintech founder charged with fraud; AI app found to be humans in the Philippines](https://techcrunch.com/2025/04/10/fintech-founder-charged-with-fraud-after-ai-shopping-app-found-to-be-powered-by-humans-in-the-philippines/)** — *Hacker News / TechCrunch* — A fintech founder faces fraud charges after their supposedly AI-powered shopping app was revealed to be run by human workers in the Philippines.

- **[Americans see their savings vanish in Synapse fintech crisis](https://www.cnbc.com/2024/11/22/synapse-bankruptcy-thousands-of-americans-see-their-savings-vanish.html)** — *Hacker News / CNBC* — Thousands of Americans lost access to their savings following the collapse of Synapse, a fintech middleware company, highlighting serious risks in Banking-as-a-Service models.

- **[Fintech is making credit cards weirder](https://workweek.com/2022/08/05/fintech-is-making-credit-cards-weirder/)** — *Hacker News / Workweek* — Fintech innovation is driving a wave of unconventional credit card products with novel rewards, structures, and underwriting approaches.

- **[Apple acquires UK open banking startup Credit Kudos](https://ffnews.com/newsarticle/apple-acquires-uk-open-banking-startup-credit-kudos/)** — *Hacker News / FF News* — Apple acquired UK-based open banking and credit scoring startup Credit Kudos, signalling the tech giant's deepening ambitions in financial services.

- **[Open Banking and Payments Competition](https://www.bitsaboutmoney.com/archive/open-banking-and-payments-competition/)** — *Hacker News / Bits About Money* — A deep-dive analysis exploring how open banking frameworks are reshaping competition in the payments landscape globally.

---

## 🍁 Top 5 Canadian Fintech News

- **[Canada calls screen scraping 'unsecure,' sets Open Banking target for 2023](https://finledger.com/2021/08/09/canada-sets-open-banking-target-for-2023/)** — *Hacker News / FinLedger* — Canada's government flagged screen scraping as a security risk and set an open banking implementation target, a milestone that continues to shape Canada's consumer-directed finance journey. *(Note: Drawn from global pool for Canadian relevance)*

- **[How the war in the Middle East is already impacting mortgage rates in Canada](https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/iran-war-mortgage-rates-canada-9.7152182?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Geopolitical instability in the Middle East is putting upward pressure on Canadian mortgage rates, with implications for household finances and lending markets.

- **[Why many of Canada's tech centaurs are in no rush to go public](https://betakit.com/why-many-of-canadas-tech-centaurs-are-in-no-rush-to-go-public/)** — *BetaKit* — Canada's billion-dollar private tech companies are choosing to stay private longer, reflecting broader trends in venture-backed startup financing strategies.

- **[Canada's next moonshot?](https://betakit.com/canadas-next-moonshot/)** — *BetaKit* — BetaKit explores what Canada's technology sector could pursue as its defining next-generation innovation challenge.

- **[Amazon adding fuel surcharge for Canadian, U.S. sellers starting April 17](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/amazon-fuel-surcharge-canada-april-17-9.7151984?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Amazon is introducing a new fuel surcharge for Canadian and American marketplace sellers beginning April 17, adding cost pressure for e-commerce businesses. *(Relevant to embedded commerce and payments for Canadian SMEs)*

---

## 🌊 Bonus: Relevant to Atlantic Fintech's Focus (1–3 articles)

- **[Fintech dystopia](https://fintechdystopia.com/)** — *Hacker News* — A thought-provoking resource documenting the unintended consequences and failures of fintech innovation, offering important lessons for responsible ecosystem building in Atlantic Canada.

- **[Towards an Open Banking API Standard](http://jackgavigan.com/2016/02/10/towards-an-open-banking-api-standard/)** — *Hacker News* — An early foundational piece on the importance of standardized open banking APIs — still highly relevant as Atlantic Canadian credit unions and fintechs prepare for Canada's open banking framework.

- **[Cerebras Backer Eclipse Raises $1.3 Billion for Robotics, AI Infrastructure](https://financialpost.com/pmn/business-pmn/cerebras-backer-eclipse-raises-1-3-billion-for-robotics-ai-infrastructure)** — *Financial Post* — Eclipse's $1.3B raise for AI and physical infrastructure startups signals strong VC appetite for deep tech, a trend Atlantic Canadian fintech founders can leverage when making their case to investors.
